translations.en: So he wept until the tears consumed the flesh of his cheeks, and the onlookers could see his molar teeth. His mother was told, and she came; Zakariyya came and the rabbis and monks gathered. They told him about the loss of his cheek flesh. He said: 'I did not feel that.' Zakariyya said: 'O my son! What calls you to this? I only asked my Lord to grant you to me to bring joy to my eyes.' He said: 'You ordered me to this, O father.' He asked: 'When was that?' He said: 'Are you not the one who said: Between Paradise and the Fire is a passage that only those who weep from the fear of Allah may cross?' He said: 'Indeed, so strive on, your path is different from mine.' Yahya stood and shook his garment. His mother said: 'Do you permit me to give you two pieces of felt to cover your teeth and dry your tears?' He said: 'Do as you please.' She gave him the felt pieces. He wept until they were soaked. He rolled up his sleeves, wrung them out, and tears dripped from his fingers. Zakariyya looked at his son and his tears, raised his head to the sky and said: 'O Allah! This is my son, these are the tears of his eyes. You are the Most Merciful of the merciful.'
